docs.title: To-day Documentation
docs.text: To-day is a simple task app that helps clear your mind. Inspired by the feeling of organizing thoughts on paper, it lets you sort tasks into today, this week, this month, or one day.

docs.title: Get started
docs.text:
New to to-day? Start with the basics:

  • App terminology — learn the words used in the app.
  • Tabs and views — understand today, this week, this month, one day, and recurring.
  • Gestures — learn tap, touch and hold, and swipe actions.

docs.title: Main features
docs.text:
• Recurring tasks
  • Task groups
  • Statistics
  • Settings

docs.title: Need help?
docs.text:
• Troubleshooting
  • Support
  • Privacy Policy

docs.title: App structure
docs.text:
To-day is organised like this:

App
→ Tab
  → View
    → Sheet

docs.title: Tab
docs.text: A main area of the app selected from the bottom menu, such as to-day, this week, this month, one day, recurring, or statistics.

docs.title: View
docs.text:
The current screen or time period shown inside a tab. Examples include Today, Yesterday, 2 days ago, Tomorrow, This Week, Last Week, This Month, and Last Month.

In the to-day tab, you can switch between to-day, yesterday, tomorrow, 2 days ago, and earlier-day views.

In the this week tab, you can switch between this week, last Week, and earlier-week views.

In the this month tab, you can switch between this month, last Month, and earlier-month views.

docs.title: Sheet
docs.text: A temporary panel that opens over the current view. Settings, Tips, and Task Info are sheets.

docs.title: Task
docs.text: An item you want to remember or complete. A task can include a title and notes, and it belongs to a planning timeframe such as today, this week, this month, or one day.

docs.title: Group
docs.text: Two or more tasks with the same group tag at the start of their titles. To-day displays them together and shows their combined progress.

docs.title: Timeframe
docs.text: When you intend to do a task: today, this week, this month, or one day.

docs.title: Recurring task
docs.text: A task set to repeat daily, weekly, or monthly. It can be enabled, disabled, archived, or deleted.

docs.title: Generated task
docs.text: A normal task created automatically from an enabled recurring task. You can complete, move, or edit it without completing the recurring task itself.

docs.title: Someday task
docs.text: A reusable task in the recurring tab that does not create tasks automatically. Use its add action whenever you want to place a copy in one of your planning tabs.

docs.title: Complete
docs.text: Marks a task as done. Completed tasks can be shown or hidden in Settings and can be marked incomplete again.

docs.title: Archive
docs.text: Hides a task while keeping it available for later restoration. Archived tasks can be viewed from Trash or shown in lists when the relevant setting is enabled.

docs.title: Delete
docs.text: Moves a task to Trash. Deleted tasks can be restored for 30 days before their identifying content is anonymised.

docs.title: Move
docs.text: Changes the task's timeframe or, within the to-day tab, moves it between the Today and Tomorrow views.

docs.title: Statistics
docs.text: Summaries and graphs in the statistics tab, based on your task history. These include completion rates, completed tasks, timing, and recurring-task streaks.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text: Short guides in the Tips sheet that demonstrate useful actions such as swipes, long presses, past views, groups, and Someday presets. Tips can be access using the lightbulb button that shows up now and then or later through the setting tab.

docs.title: To-day at a glance
docs.text:
To-day is a todo app that clears your mind. Inspired by the simplicity of organizing thoughts on paper, it helps you sort tasks into today, this week, this month, or one day.

to-day view at a glance

Once it’s written down, it’s out of your head. So you can focus on doing, not remembering. Recurring tasks, ticking things off, it’s all designed to feel natural and simple. Write it down, and trust it’s there.

Its main tabs are:

  • to-day for the Today, Yesterday, and Tomorrow views.
  • this week for tasks you plan to do during the current week.
  • this month for tasks you plan to do during the current month.
  • one day for tasks without a near-term plan.
  • recurring for tasks that repeat or reusable presets.
  • statistics for completion summaries, graphs, and recurring-task progress.

Use the other pages in this guide to learn about tabs and views, task actions, groups, recurring tasks, statistics, settings, and recovery options.

docs.title: Tap to edit
docs.text: Tap a task title to edit it. Finish editing to save the updated title.

docs.title: Long press for Task Info
docs.text: Press and hold a task to open the Task Info sheet. From there you can review details, add notes, rename the task, change its planning tab or planned date, and access other available actions.

docs.title: Swipe right
docs.text: Swipe a task to the right to reveal actions such as complete or mark incomplete. The available shortcuts depend on the task, tab, and view you are using.

docs.title: Swipe left
docs.text: Swipe a task to the left to reveal actions such as archive, move to a later timeframe, or delete.

docs.title: Half-swipe actions
docs.text:
A partial swipe reveals the available shortcuts without immediately performing a full-swipe action. This is useful when you want to choose a specific move or information action.

Because actions change by tab and view, check the labels before selecting one.

docs.title: Recurring Tasks
docs.text: Recurring tasks help with tasks that repeats. Create and manage them in the recurring tab.

docs.title: Frequencies
docs.text:
To-day supports:

  • Daily, which creates tasks in the Today and Tomorrow view of the to-day tab.
  • Weekly, which creates tasks in the This Week view of the this week tab.
  • Monthly, which creates tasks in the This Month view of the this month tab.
  • Someday, which acts as a reusable preset for tasks you create when needed.

Editing a recurring task updates the tasks generated from it. Changes to the title, notes, or timing are passed on to linked generated tasks.

If a generated task has already been edited individually, To-day keeps that task’s custom change.

docs.title: Active and inactive
docs.text:
An enabled recurring task continues creating its scheduled tasks. Disable it to pause new generated tasks without deleting the recurring task.

You can enable or disable a recurring task by taping its icon or by swiping right.

docs.title: Generated tasks
docs.text:
A generated task appears in the appropriate planning tab and view. Treat it like a normal task: complete it, move it, archive it, or edit it.

Editing a generated task can make that occurrence different from its recurring task. For example, you can rename one generated task without changing every future occurrence.

docs.title: Someday
docs.text: Setting a recurring task to someday doesn't create tasks automatically. Tap the add action on a task or group of task to create a task in the to-day, tomorrow, this week, this month, or one day tab.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Open the Settings sheet to adjust how to-day displays tasks and defines your planning periods.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
Choose how completed and incomplete tasks are arranged, or leave the list unsorted. This also moves the "create task button".

Top | Bottom | Unsorted

docs.title: Coloured status circle
docs.text:
The coloured circle shows when if a task was or will be completed in the intended window.

  • Green: active in the current view, or completed in its intended timeframe.
  • Blue: planned for tomorrow, or completed ahead of time.
  • Red: overdue, or completed after its intended timeframe.
  • Grey: from a past locked view.
  • Soft grey: archived.

You can turn coloured status circles off. When off, active tasks appear grey and completed tasks appear green.

docs.title: Day, week, and month boundaries
docs.text:
• Day starts at changes when to-day moves from one logical day to the next.
  • Week starts on selects the first day of your week.
  • Month starts on selects the day that begins your planning month.

These choices affect which tasks appear in the current and previous time-period views.

docs.title: Task visibility
docs.text: Choose whether to show completed tasks, creation dates, completion dates, and archived tasks.

docs.title: Tips and Info
docs.text: The Tips sheet provides short demonstrations of useful app actions. Info contains details about the app.

docs.title: Trash
docs.text: The Trash view contains archived tasks and recently deleted tasks. You can restore items, move archived tasks into Trash, or permanently remove data using the available confirmed actions.

docs.title: Storage summary
docs.text: The Settings sheet shows the number of tasks stored and the size of the local app data. iCloud storage is managed by iCloud and is not shown there.

docs.title: Statistics
docs.text: The statistics tab gives you an overview of how tasks move from planning to completion.

docs.title: Completion rates
docs.text: The all-time overview shows the share of stored tasks you have completed. Smaller rings show completion rates for the to-day, this week, this month, and one day tabs.

docs.title: Progress and completed tasks
docs.text: Graphs compare created and completed tasks over time. The completed-per-day section can show This Week, This Month, or This Year, together with a weekly average and best day total.

docs.title: Completion time and timing
docs.text: To-day estimates how long tasks usually remain open and summarises whether completed tasks were finished before, during, or after their planned timeframe.

docs.title: Insights
docs.text: Insights highlight patterns such as how many tasks you complete on active days, how long tasks stay open, and how often tasks move between timeframes.

docs.title: Recurring progress and streaks
docs.text: Recurring statistics can show completion rates, current streaks, and best streaks for enabled daily, weekly, and monthly recurring tasks. Someday presets are counted by how often they create tasks rather than by streak.

docs.title: Task Groups
docs.text: Task groups collect related tasks and show their combined progress.

docs.title: Create a group
docs.text:
Add the same group tag at the beginning of at least two task titles. You can use square brackets, curly braces, or colons:

[Home] Buy milk
[Home] Clean kitchen

You can also write {Home} or :Home:. Tasks with matching group names are displayed together.

docs.title: Group progress
docs.text: The group row shows how many tasks it contains and visually reflects the completion state of those tasks. Open the group by tapping on it and to see and manage each task.

docs.title: Add a task inside a group
docs.text: Open the group and use its add-task control. To-day adds the group tag to the new task so it remains inside the group.

docs.title: Group suggestions
docs.text:
Start a new task with an existing group tag to add it to that group.

Once you have created at least one group, to-day will suggest matching group tags when you type [, { or :.

See Create a group.

docs.title: Rename a group
docs.text:
To rename a group, touch and hold the group title. Enter the new group name and save.

Renaming a group updates the group name used by the tasks inside it, so they stay together under the new name.

This affects only the tasks in the current timeframe.

docs.title: I cannot see a recurring task
docs.text:
Open the recurring tab and check that the recurring task is enabled and not archived. Daily, weekly, and monthly recurring tasks appear in their matching planning tab and view. Someday presets do not create tasks automatically; use the add action on the preset.

Also check the relevant tab and view, including the Tomorrow view or a previous time-period view if the task was moved or scheduled there.

docs.title: I moved a task by mistake
docs.text: If the undo message is still visible, tap Undo. Otherwise, open the Task Info sheet or use the available swipe action to move the task back to the intended timeframe.

docs.title: I deleted or archived a task
docs.text:
Open the Settings sheet, then select Trash.

  • Restore an archived task from the Archived list.
  • Restore a recently deleted task from the Deleted list.
  • Deleted tasks remain restorable for 30 days before their identifying content is anonymised.

docs.title: My tasks look different on another device
docs.text:
Make sure both devices use the same Apple Account for iCloud and that iCloud is available. Allow time for changes to sync, then reopen to-day on both devices.

Settings can also change what is visible. Compare options such as Show completed tasks and Show archived tasks in the Settings sheet on each device.

docs.title: I cannot find a group
docs.text:
A group appears when at least two visible tasks begin with the same group tag. Check that the tag is at the start of each title and uses a supported format, for example:

[Home] First task
[Home] Second task

Also check whether one of the tasks was moved, completed and hidden, archived, or deleted.

docs.title: To-day tab
docs.text: The to-day tab contains the Today, past-day, and Tomorrow views. Use the title arrows to switch between them.

docs.title: To-day view
docs.text: The to-day view contains tasks planned for the current day.

docs.title: Tomorrow view
docs.text: The tomorrow view lets you plan the next day while keeping those tasks separate from the Today view.

docs.title: Past-day views
docs.text: Use the left title arrow to review Yesterday, 2 days ago, and earlier-day views. Older views may limit actions that would change historical completion information.

docs.title: This week tab
docs.text: The this week tab contains the this Week, last Week, and earlier-week views. The This Week view is for tasks you intend to handle during the current week.

docs.title: This month tab
docs.text: The this month tab contains the this Month, last Month, and earlier-month views. The This Month view is for tasks that matter during the current month but do not yet belong in the Today or This Week views.

docs.title: One day tab
docs.text: The one day tab is for tasks you want to keep without committing them to the current day, week, or month.

docs.title: Recurring tab
docs.text: The recurring tab contains repeating tasks and Someday presets. Daily, weekly, and monthly tasks can create tasks in the matching planning tab. Someday presets create a task only when you choose to add one.

docs.title: Statistics tab
docs.text: The statistics tab shows completion summaries, graphs, timing information, planning insights, and recurring-task progress.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: The Settings sheet controls task layout, date boundaries, visibility options, and access to Tips, app information, storage summaries, and Trash.

docs.title: Trash view and archived tasks
docs.text: Open Settings > Trash to open the Trash sheet. It contains archived tasks and recently deleted tasks. Archived tasks remain available until restored or deleted. Deleted tasks can be restored for 30 days.

docs.title: Icon glossary
docs.text:
This page explains the main icons used in to-day.

The icons below are shown as they appear in the app. Some icons may change colour depending on the task state, selected tab, or whether a task is archived, completed, or being edited.

docs.title: Main navigation
docs.text:
These icons appear in the main navigation bar and settings areas.

Today

or Daily

Your main tasks: the things you want to get done today.

This Week

or Weekly

Your first level of planning: the things you want to complete this week.

This Month

or Monthly

Your next level of planning: the things you want to do soon, but not yet.

One Day

or Someday

Your backlog: the things you will do, but not now.

Recurring

The things you do often, repeated daily, weekly, or monthly.

Statistics

Your completion history, streaks, and progress information.

Settings

App settings and preferences.

docs.title: Task actions
docs.text:
These icons appear on task rows, swipe actions, task information screens, and edit controls.

Complete

Marks a task as done.

Complete

Marks a task as not done.

Archive

Hides a task without deleting it. Archived tasks can be shown again later.

Unarchive

Restores an archived task.

Delete

Deletes the task.

Task info

Opens the task information sheet.

Task info — edit mode

Shows that the task information sheet is currently editable.

docs.title: Moving tasks
docs.text:
These icons are used when moving a task from one view to another.

Move to to-day

Moves the task to the to-day view.

Move to Tomorrow

Moves the task to tomorrow.

Move to This Week

Moves the task to the current week.

Move to This Month

Moves the task to the current month.

Move to One Day

Moves the task to One Day, for things you want to do later.

docs.title: Copying and moving tasks
docs.text:
Some task actions use the same destination icons, but the small + badge changes the meaning.

Copy to creates another version of the task in the selected destination. The original task stays where it is.

Use Copy to when you want the same task to exist in more than one place, for example copying a task from One Day into to-day while keeping it in your backlog.

Move to sends the task to the selected destination. The task is removed from its current view and appears in the new one.

Use Move to when the task belongs somewhere else, for example moving a task from This Month to This Week when you are ready to plan it.

docs.title: Icon colour meanings
docs.text:
Some icons use colour to communicate state.

Colour Meaning Green Active, current, or completed Blue Planned for tomorrow Red Overdue or destructive action Grey Inactive, past, disabled, or archived

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text:
Last updated: 13 June 2026

To-day is designed to be a private todo app.

You can create, edit, complete, archive, and delete your own tasks.

Your task data is stored on your device.

When iCloud is available, to-day uses Apple iCloud and CloudKit to sync your tasks across your own devices.

docs.title: Data
docs.text:
To-day does not:

  • Sell your data
  • Use advertising trackers
  • Use third-party analytics
  • Store your task content on my own server
  • Share your task content with third parties

docs.title: iCloud sync
docs.text:
iCloud sync is provided by Apple through iCloud and CloudKit. Sync depends on your Apple ID, iCloud settings, device settings, and network connection.

Task data synced through iCloud is handled by Apple’s iCloud and CloudKit services.

docs.title: Support emails
docs.text:
If you contact support by email, I will receive the information you choose to send. This may include your email address, message, device details, screenshots, or bug report information.

I will only use this information to reply to you and help with your request.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
For privacy questions, support, or feedback, email:

acreage_swoons_88@icloud.com

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
Questions, feedback, and bug reports are welcome.

Email: acreage_swoons_88@icloud.com

docs.title: Bug reports
docs.text:
If something is not working as expected, please include:

  • Your device model
  • Your iOS or iPadOS version
  • The version of to-day you are using
  • What happened
  • What you were doing just before it happened

docs.title: iCloud sync
docs.text: To-day uses iCloud and CloudKit to sync tasks when available. Sync can depend on your Apple ID, iCloud settings, device settings, and network connection.
